name: anethole
summary: Phenylpropene from fennel and anise associated with digestive and anti-inflammatory actions.
description: Phenylpropene from fennel and anise associated with digestive and anti-inflammatory actions.
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated for most users.
contraindications: Pregnancy/breastfeeding for concentrated essential-oil use (traditional emmenagogue/uterine-stimulant use)
infants and young children (concentrated anise/fennel essential-oil preparations linked to case reports of neurotoxicity and seizures)
hormone-sensitive conditions without clinician review (phytoestrogenic activity reported)
Potential additive effects with hormone therapies given estrogenic activity
theoretical CYP-mediated interactions at high essential-oil doses
avoid combining with other photosensitizing or hepatotoxic essential oils.
